summary: Official company materials show Axsome Therapeutics acquired the SUNOSI product from Jazz Pharmaceuticals in 2022, but neither company acquired, merged with, or became a subsidiary of the other. This is an asset/product divestiture transaction, so the company-to-company relationship is best classified as None/Unclear under the provided rules.

Current official-source evidence shows a transaction in which Axsome obtained exclusive global rights to balipodect (TAK-063) from Takeda under an asset purchase agreement, with Takeda receiving upfront, milestone, and royalty payments. This is an asset/licensing-style deal, not a company acquisition, merger, or parent-subsidiary relationship between Axsome and Takeda.
SEC-filed subsidiary lists for Axsome Therapeutics include Axsome Malta Ltd., and Axsome Therapeutics' 2024 annual report describes Axsome Malta as a wholly-owned subsidiary of the company.

Current and historical SEC Exhibit 21.1 subsidiary lists for Axsome Therapeutics, Inc. identify "Axsome Therapeutics, Limited" as a subsidiary in Ireland. This is strong official evidence of a parent-subsidiary relationship.

Current official disclosures state that Axsome Therapeutics acquired 100% of the equity of Baergic Bio, Inc. in November 2025. Avenue Therapeutics and SEC filings describe Baergic Bio as having been acquired by Axsome, making the relationship an acquisition.

Current and historical SEC filings for Axsome Therapeutics list Axsome Therapeutics Australia Pty Ltd as a subsidiary of the company, and a 2022 company filing also explicitly defines it as the "Australian Subsidiary."

Recent SEC Exhibit 21.1 filings for Axsome Therapeutics, Inc. list 'Axsome Therapeutics, Limited' in Ireland under 'Subsidiaries of the Company,' indicating the Ireland entity is a subsidiary of Axsome Therapeutics, Inc.

Axsome Therapeutics, Inc.'s SEC annual report lists 'Axsome International Holdings LLC' in Exhibit 21.1 as one of the company's subsidiaries, which is direct official evidence of a parent-subsidiary relationship.

Current official Axsome pages describe Axsome as an independent biopharmaceutical company, and current official Takeda news/partnership pages do not show Axsome as an acquired company, merger partner, or subsidiary. Search results also did not surface any official Axsome-Takeda acquisition, merger, or parent-subsidiary announcement. At most, any apparent connection is indirect or absent, so the relationship should be classified as None/Unclear.

Official SEC filings state that in November 2013, Cyrenaic Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and Sonkei Pharmaceuticals, Inc. merged, and the combined company was renamed Minerva Neurosciences, Inc. This supports a merger relationship rather than an acquisition or subsidiary structure.

SEC filings and recent company-related filings state that Minerva Neurosciences acquired Mind-NRG Sarl in February 2014, and more recent filings describe Mind-NRG SARL as a wholly owned subsidiary of Minerva Neurosciences.

Recent SEC filings by Minerva Neurosciences, Inc. state that its consolidated financial statements include its wholly-owned subsidiaries, specifically naming Minerva Neurosciences Securities Corporation. Older annual report exhibits also list Minerva Neurosciences Securities Corporation as a subsidiary of Minerva Neurosciences, Inc.

Official SEC filings from Minerva Neurosciences state that it acquired Mind-NRG Sarl in February 2014 and that Mind-NRG Sarl is included among Minerva's wholly-owned subsidiaries in the 2025 Form 10-K. Because the current corporate relationship is requested, the best resolution is Subsidiary rather than Acquisition.

Official SEC filings show Minerva Neurosciences was originally incorporated as Cyrenaic Pharmaceuticals, Inc. in 2007 and later renamed Minerva Neurosciences, Inc. after Cyrenaic merged with Sonkei Pharmaceuticals in November 2013. That means Cyrenaic is the former name/legal predecessor of Minerva, not a separate company acquired by or merged with Minerva as a distinct counterparty.

Pfizer announced in March 2023 that it would acquire Seagen, and Pfizer later announced it completed the acquisition in December 2023. Official Pfizer pages explicitly describe the transaction as an acquisition of Seagen by Pfizer.

Pfizer announced in August 2022 that it would acquire Global Blood Therapeutics, and later announced it had completed the acquisition in October 2022. This is clear acquisition evidence from Pfizer's official press releases.

Pfizer officially announced in 2015 that it would acquire Hospira, and Pfizer later stated that it completed the acquisition. Regulatory materials from the FTC also describe the transaction as Pfizer's acquisition of Hospira.

Pfizer officially announced in January 2009 that it would acquire Wyeth, and later announced that Pfizer and Wyeth had become one company upon completion of the transaction. FTC materials also describe the deal as Pfizer's acquisition of Wyeth.

Official SEC-filed press release states Pfizer and Metsera entered into a definitive agreement on September 22, 2025 under which Pfizer would acquire Metsera. Pfizer’s 2025 Form 10-K then states Pfizer acquired Metsera on November 13, 2025. FTC HSR records also list Pfizer as acquiring party and Metsera as acquired party.

Official Pfizer press releases state that Pfizer announced and then completed the acquisition of Biohaven Pharmaceuticals in October 2022. SEC filings further document an Agreement and Plan of Merger under which Pfizer acquired all outstanding common shares of Biohaven Pharmaceutical Holding Company Ltd. not already owned by Pfizer.
Official Pfizer press releases state that Pfizer announced an agreement to acquire Global Blood Therapeutics for $5.4 billion in August 2022 and later completed the acquisition in October 2022.

Pfizer announced in December 2021 that it would acquire Arena Pharmaceuticals for approximately $6.7 billion, and SEC filing evidence states the transaction was completed on March 11, 2022, when Pfizer acquired all outstanding Arena shares.

Official Pfizer press releases state that Pfizer agreed to acquire Biohaven and later completed the acquisition in October 2022. Biohaven's SEC filing also describes the transaction as a merger in which Biohaven became a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer, supporting that the overall relationship is an acquisition.
